literal_meaning: placing together or bringing into composure
definition: A state of mental composure in which the mind is steady and well coordinated.
notes: Composure reflects steadiness and collectedness without implying forced concentration. This choice now extends into related compounds such as sammā-samādhi, where the project likewise prefers right composure over right concentration. Ven. Dr. M. Punnaji often uses mental equilibrium and stillness of mind in meditative and path-factor exposition; the repository records those as source-backed alternates while retaining composure as the house default. The OSF glossary explains sam?dhi as unification of mind and composure; the repository records unification of mind as a source-backed explanatory alternate while keeping composure as the default.

context_rules.context: In prose emphasizing the gathered quality of mind
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when it reads more naturally.

context_rules.context: In Punnaji-style pedagogical meditation prose emphasizing calm balance of mind
context_rules.notes: Source-backed alternate, not the house default.
